headline: Vertical Video Pioneer RoseBerry Media Aims to Revolutionize Content Repurposing
lede: A new startup helmed by television and technology veterans is poised to transform how international content libraries get reimagined for mobile-first audiences. RoseBerry Media launches with strategic distribution partnerships already locked in.
body:
The vertical video market just scored a major player with RoseBerry Media's debut, signaling a potential sea change in how global entertainment content gets repackaged for digital consumption.

Founded by a consortium of industry veterans including Abot Hameiri creator Guy Hamieri, the startup specializes in transforming traditional television content into mobile-optimized vertical formats. RoseBerry's core strategy centers on working directly with international program distributors to breathe new life into existing library catalogs.

The emerging platform represents a critical pivot point for content owners struggling to engage younger demographics increasingly consuming media through smartphone screens. By leveraging advanced editing technologies and algorithmic content adaptation, RoseBerry can rapidly convert long-form programming into bite-sized vertical segments designed for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and similar short-form platforms.

Technology will be central to RoseBerry's approach, with proprietary AI-driven editing tools that can intelligently extract narrative arcs and compelling visual moments from longer source materials. The company's founding team brings deep expertise from television production, streaming technology, and digital media investment sectors.

While specific distribution deals remain under wraps, industry insiders suggest RoseBerry could dramatically expand monetization opportunities for content libraries that might otherwise languish in archival storage.
key_facts: Founded by Guy Hamieri and tech veterans
Specializes in vertical video content conversion
Aims to revitalize existing content libraries
